name: Flip Chess
author: John William Brown (1998) — AGP port
description: Flip Chess (John William Brown, 1998; second prize in Hans Bodlaender's 38-square challenge). On a 7x6 board with its four corners removed (38 cells), every piece is a double-sided counter: a Pawn flips to a Berolina pawn, a Bishop to a Rook, a Ferz to a Knight, either at the close of its move or as a move in itself. Pawns promote to a non-royal Prince. Win by checkmate or by baring the enemy King. The 'Flip Shogi' option adds shogi-style drops (drop either side up; a drop must threaten an enemy piece; pawn drops stay in your first two ranks).
